result:
Because by default React re-renders all descendants when a parent re-renders, regardless of whether their props changed.

Fixes:
- Wrap the child in `React.memo(Component)` — skips re-render if props are shallow-equal.
- Stabilize prop references: wrap functions in `useCallback`, objects/arrays in `useMemo`. Inline `{}`, `[]`, or `() => {}` create new references every render and defeat `memo`.
- Pull unrelated state out of the parent, or move the child to accept `children` (children passed from above don't re-render when the parent's own state changes).
- For context consumers: split contexts so updates only fan out to components that care.

Quickest check: add `console.log` in the child and inspect props with React DevTools' "Highlight re-renders" to see which prop is changing identity.
